name: Archestra
what_it_does: Cloud-native platform that serves as a centralized MCP registry, gateway, and orchestrator with enterprise governance, security controls, cost monitoring, and prompt injection defense for managing MCP servers across organizations.
use_cases: Centralized management of MCP servers across an organization
Enterprise governance and access control for AI agent tooling
Cost monitoring and optimization for AI agent operations
Security-hardened MCP gateway with prompt injection defense
Team-level tool sharing with credential management
Observability and audit trails for AI agent tool usage
not_for: Individual developers who just want to run a single MCP server
Non-containerized environments without Docker or Kubernetes
Teams looking for a simple MCP server rather than an orchestration platform
best_when: You are managing multiple MCP servers across teams in an organization and need centralized governance, security, cost controls, and observability.
avoid_when: You are an individual developer running one or two MCP servers locally - the overhead of a full orchestration platform is not justified.

auth.notes: Manages API keys and OAuth for downstream MCP servers. Provides per-team and per-agent credential management. The platform itself handles credential orchestration for connected MCP servers.

pricing.notes: AGPL-3.0 licensed. Self-hosted and free. Likely has commercial offerings but not documented in the README. Docker quickstart available for evaluation.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Documentation focuses on platform capabilities rather than error handling specifics. Observability features (metrics, traces, logs) suggest good operational visibility but error response format for agents is not documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Platform is a gateway/orchestrator - idempotency depends on downstream MCP servers.
known_agent_gotchas: Requires Docker and container orchestration knowledge to deploy
AGPL-3.0 license has copyleft implications for derivative works
Claims 96% cost reduction via dynamic model optimization - verify independently
Claims non-probabilistic security via dual LLM architecture - novel and unproven approach
2,353 commits and 138 releases suggests active development but also potential instability
Documentation is more marketing-oriented than technically detailed for integration

security_score_components.security_notes: Community/specialized tool. Apply standard security practices for category. Review documentation for specific security requirements.
